---
title: "Chapter 6 - Atma-Samyama Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">आत्मसंयमयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-05.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-07.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-6-1" data-reference="6.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-1">6.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who performs the action that ought to be done, not depending on its fruit, is a renunciant and a yogin—not the one who lights no fire and does no rite.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनाश्रितः कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति यः ।<br>
स संन्यासी च योगी च न निरग्निर्न चाक्रियः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-2" data-reference="6.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-2">6.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Know that what they call renunciation is <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." aria-label="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." title="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." data-glossary-id="pandava">Pandava</span>; for none becomes a yogin without renouncing self-will.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यं संन्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव ।<br>
न ह्यसंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-3" data-reference="6.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-3">6.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For the sage who seeks to rise to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, action is said to be the means;<br>
for one who has risen to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, stillness is said to be the means.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म कारणमुच्यते ।<br>
योगारूढस्य तस्यैव शमः कारणमुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-4" data-reference="6.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-4">6.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When one is attached neither to the objects of sense nor to actions, having renounced all self-will, then one is said to have risen to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते ।<br>
सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-5" data-reference="6.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-5">6.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Let one lift oneself by oneself; let one not drag oneself down. For oneself alone is one&#x27;s friend, and oneself alone one&#x27;s enemy.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत् ।<br>
आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-6" data-reference="6.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-6">6.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For one who has conquered himself by himself, the self is a friend to the self; but for one who has not mastered himself, the self stays hostile, like an enemy.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बन्धुरात्मात्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जितः ।<br>
अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-7" data-reference="6.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-7">6.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For one who has mastered himself and is at peace, the supreme <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." aria-label="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." title="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." data-glossary-id="atman">Atman</span> stays steady, alike in cold and heat, pleasure and pain, honor and dishonor.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः ।<br>
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-8" data-reference="6.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-8">6.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The yogin content in knowledge and realization, unwavering, his senses subdued, to whom a clod, a stone, and gold are alike, is said to be yoked.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रियः ।<br>
युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-9" data-reference="6.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-9">6.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who is even-minded toward well-wisher, friend, and foe, toward the neutral and the mediator, toward the hateful and the kinsman, and toward the good and the wicked alike, stands supreme.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु ।<br>
साधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-10" data-reference="6.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-10">6.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Let the yogin discipline himself constantly, staying in solitude, alone, his mind and self controlled, free of longing and of possessions.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थितः ।<br>
एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रहः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-11" data-reference="6.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-11">6.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having set up a firm seat for himself in a clean place, neither too high nor too low, covered with cloth, deerskin, and kusha grass,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मनः ।<br>
नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-12" data-reference="6.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-12">6.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">there, making the mind one-pointed, the workings of mind and senses checked, seated on that seat, let him practice <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> to purify the self.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तत्रैकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः ।<br>
उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-13" data-reference="6.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-13">6.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Holding body, head, and neck erect and still, steady, gazing at the tip of his own nose without looking about,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिरः ।<br>
सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-14" data-reference="6.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-14">6.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">his mind serene and free of fear, firm in the vow of celibacy, the mind controlled and thought fixed on me, let him sit, yoked, intent on me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थितः ।<br>
मनः संयम्य मच्चित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्परः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-15" data-reference="6.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-15">6.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thus ever disciplining himself, the yogin of controlled mind attains the peace that culminates in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." aria-label="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." title="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." data-glossary-id="nirvana">nirvana</span> and abides in me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी नियतमानसः ।<br>
शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-16" data-reference="6.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-16">6.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">Yoga</span> is not for one who eats too much, nor for one who eats nothing at all, nor for one given to too much sleep, nor for one always awake, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नात्यश्नतस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्नतः ।<br>
न चातिस्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्जुन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-17" data-reference="6.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-17">6.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For one measured in food and recreation, measured in his actions, measured in sleep and waking, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> becomes the destroyer of sorrow.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु ।<br>
युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दुःखहा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-18" data-reference="6.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-18">6.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When the disciplined mind comes to rest in the self alone, free of longing for any desire, then one is said to be yoked.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते ।<br>
निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-19" data-reference="6.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-19">6.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">As a lamp in a windless place does not flicker—such is the image of the yogin of controlled mind, practicing the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of the self.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता ।<br>
योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-20" data-reference="6.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-20">6.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When the mind, restrained by the practice of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, comes to rest, and seeing the self by the self, one is content within the self;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया ।<br>
यत्र चैवात्मनात्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-21" data-reference="6.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-21">6.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">when one knows that boundless happiness, grasped by the understanding and beyond the senses, and, established in it, never strays from the truth;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सुखमात्यन्तिकं यत्तद् बुद्धिग्राह्यमतीन्द्रियम् ।<br>
वेत्ति यत्र न चैवायं स्थितश्चलति तत्त्वतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-22" data-reference="6.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-22">6.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">and, having gained it, counts no other gain greater—established in it, he is unshaken even by the heaviest sorrow:</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं ततः ।<br>
यस्मिन्स्थितो न दुःखेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-23" data-reference="6.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-23">6.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">let it be known that this severance of all union with sorrow is what is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>; that <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> is to be practiced with resolve and an undismayed mind.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तं विद्याद् दुःखसंयोगवियोगं योगसंज्ञितम् ।<br>
स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-24" data-reference="6.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-24">6.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Abandoning without remainder all desires born of self-will, restraining the whole throng of senses on every side by the mind alone,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषतः ।<br>
मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्ततः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-25" data-reference="6.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-25">6.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">little by little let him grow still, the understanding held firm; fixing the mind on the self, let him think of nothing at all.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शनैः शनैरुपरमेद् बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया ।<br>
आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-26" data-reference="6.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-26">6.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Wherever the restless, unsteady mind wanders off, from there let him rein it back under the control of the self.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम् ।<br>
ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-27" data-reference="6.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-27">6.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For supreme happiness comes to the yogin whose mind is at peace, whose passion is stilled, who is free of taint and has become <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम् ।<br>
उपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-28" data-reference="6.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-28">6.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thus ever disciplining himself, the yogin, freed of taint, easily attains the boundless happiness of contact with <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी विगतकल्मषः ।<br>
सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-29" data-reference="6.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-29">6.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">His self yoked in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, he sees the self present in all beings and all beings in the self, seeing the same everywhere.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि ।<br>
ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-30" data-reference="6.30">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-30">6.30</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For one who sees me everywhere and sees all things in me, I am not lost to him, nor is he lost to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति ।<br>
तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-31" data-reference="6.31">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-31">6.31</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The yogin who, established in oneness, worships me as dwelling in all beings abides in me, in whatever way he lives.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थितः ।<br>
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-32" data-reference="6.32">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-32">6.32</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who sees by analogy with himself the same everywhere, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>, whether in pleasure or in pain, is held to be the highest yogin.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन ।<br>
सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं स योगी परमो मतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-33" data-reference="6.33">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-33">6.33</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of evenness you have taught, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." aria-label="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." title="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." data-glossary-id="madhusudana">Madhusudana</span>, I do not see how it can stand firm, because the mind is so restless.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन ।<br>
एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात्स्थितिं स्थिराम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-34" data-reference="6.34">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-34">6.34</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For the mind is restless, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>—turbulent, strong, and unyielding; to restrain it, I think, is as hard as to restrain the wind.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद् दृढम् ।<br>
तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-35" data-reference="6.35">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-35">6.35</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">No doubt, O mighty-armed one, the mind is hard to restrain and restless; but by practice, O son of Kunti, and by dispassion it is held in check.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम् ।<br>
अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-36" data-reference="6.36">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-36">6.36</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">Yoga</span> is hard to reach, I agree, for one whose self is unrestrained; but for one who strives, self-controlled, it can be attained by the right means.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मतिः ।<br>
वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-37" data-reference="6.37">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-37">6.37</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One of faith who does not strive, whose mind has strayed from <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> and failed to reach its perfection—what end does he meet, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अयतिः श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्चलितमानसः ।<br>
अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-38" data-reference="6.38">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-38">6.38</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Fallen from both, does he not perish like a scattered cloud, without support, O mighty-armed one, bewildered on the path of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कच्चिन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति ।<br>
अप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मणः पथि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-39" data-reference="6.39">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-39">6.39</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">You are fit, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>, to dispel this doubt of mine completely; for none but you can be found to remove it.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषतः ।<br>
त्वदन्यः संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-40" data-reference="6.40">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-40">6.40</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, neither here nor hereafter is there ruin for him; for no one who does good, my friend, ever comes to a bad end.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते ।<br>
न हि कल्याणकृत्कश्चिद् दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-41" data-reference="6.41">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-41">6.41</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having reached the worlds of those who do right and dwelt there countless years, the one fallen from <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> is born in the house of the pure and prosperous;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वतीः समाः ।<br>
शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-42" data-reference="6.42">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-42">6.42</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">or else he is born into a family of wise yogins—though a birth like this is harder still to win in this world.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम् ।<br>
एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-43" data-reference="6.43">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-43">6.43</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">There he regains the union of understanding gained in his former body, and from there strives once more toward perfection, O joy of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kuru: The Kuru lineage or its members; context may refer to the wider dynasty or the Kaurava side." aria-label="Kuru: The Kuru lineage or its members; context may refer to the wider dynasty or the Kaurava side." title="Kuru: The Kuru lineage or its members; context may refer to the wider dynasty or the Kaurava side." data-glossary-id="kuru">Kurus</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम् ।<br>
यतते च ततो भूयः संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-44" data-reference="6.44">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-44">6.44</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">By that very former practice he is carried onward, even against his will; even wishing to know <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, he passes beyond the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> of mere words.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पूर्वाभ्यासेन तेनैव ह्रियते ह्यवशोऽपि सः ।<br>
जिज्ञासुरपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्मातिवर्तते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-45" data-reference="6.45">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-45">6.45</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But the yogin who strives with effort, cleansed of all faults, perfected through many births, then reaches the supreme goal.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रयत्नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिषः ।<br>
अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-46" data-reference="6.46">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-46">6.46</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The yogin is greater than the ascetics, greater, it is held, than the men of knowledge, and greater than the men of action; therefore become a yogin, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तपस्विभ्योऽधिको योगी ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि मतोऽधिकः ।<br>
कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिको योगी तस्माद्योगी भवार्जुन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-47" data-reference="6.47">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-47">6.47</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And of all yogins, the one who, full of faith, worships me with his inmost self drawn to me—him I hold to be the most deeply yoked.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना ।<br>
श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
